title: FORVIA and Sinopec Capital partner to accelerate hydrogen growth in China
content: FORVIA announced a minority investment of RMB 300 million (â‰ˆâ‚¬40 million) in its hydrogenâ€‘focused subsidiary, Forvia Hydrogen Solutions China, through strategic backing from Sinopec Capital via its Chaoyang Hydrogen New Energy Venture Capital fund. This collaboration strengthens Forviaâ€™s presence in Chinaâ€™s fastâ€‘growing hydrogen market, which benefits from robust government policies integrating hydrogen into the national energy management system alongside gasoline and natural gas. In 2024, China produced 36.5 million tons of hydrogen, a 3.5 percent increase over 2023, primarily used in chemicals, with expanding application in transport and steel, and became the worldâ€™s largest market for hydrogen fuel cell vehicles with over 30,000 sold and 559 refueling stations. The 2025 roadmap sets targets of half a million hydrogen vehicles by 2030 and over one million by 2035, supported by subsidies, toll exemptions, and lower hydrogen prices. Partnering with Sinopec Capital, a leader in Chinaâ€™s hydrogen value chain, provides Forvia Hydrogen Solutions China access to key government contracts, industrial synergies, and optimized supply chains including carbon fiber and resins. Ma Chuan, Forviaâ€™s Executive Vice President and President of Faurecia China, highlighted accelerated market access and cost competitiveness to lead globally in hydrogen solutions, while SÃ©bastien Limousin, Executive Vice President of Forvia Clean Mobility, emphasized Chinaâ€™s leadership in sustainable mobility. Ma Ming, Chairman of Sinopec Capital, noted their commitment to becoming Chinaâ€™s number one hydrogen company and achieving winâ€‘win outcomes to support the global hydrogen industry
